title: monarda fistulosa var. stipitatoglandulosa
intro: Monarda fistulosa var. stipitatoglandulosa, also known as Bee Balm or Wild Bergamot, is a perennial herb native to North America, characterized by its red to purple flowers and distinctive scent reminiscent of bergamot. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high content of essential oils, particularly linalool and thymol. Bee Balm has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, sore throats, and skin irritations, as well as to alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, salves, and infusions, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
